name: ritual-chamber
summary: A consecrated space for invocations and summoning — a central figure (circle, altar, or pool) with radial focus, candles and braziers defining the working floor.

primary_palette.note: summoning ground — circle ringed by candles at cardinal points
altar at the head of the circle — an offering-and-invocation setup
necromantic rite — bones at the corners, altar in the heart
ley-line focus — four obelisks channeling into a central pedestal
past ritual — the bodies are still there

story_prompts: active ritual interrupted — candles still lit, chalk still fresh, the caster fled
ancient chamber, runes faded but the geometry still works
bound-demon chamber where the circle holds something still
preparatory state — robes hung, braziers cold, the rite is tomorrow
ruined ritual — the circle was broken and something got out
anti_patterns: don't fill the circle with props — its power is its emptiness
don't use warm hearth light; ritual chambers are cold-lit
avoid furniture that doesn't serve the rite (no mess tables, no beds)
